body:
# Summary

Consolidate `gh` CLI JSON payload parsing into a single validation layer (using `zod`) that lives in `packages/core` and is consumed by every `gh --json` call site across `packages/core`, `packages/cli`, and `packages/mcp`. Eliminate duplicated and unvalidated parsers so malformed `gh` output produces a single, consistent, field-level error, and so that GitHub schema changes require updating one place.

This is a robustness and maintainability refactor — no user-facing feature changes on well-formed payloads.

# Requirements

1. A single validation module in `packages/core` exposes one canonical parser per `gh --json` payload shape currently used by Shipper. Every `gh --json` consumer in `packages/core`, `packages/cli`, and `packages/mcp` uses the parser for its shape. No ad-hoc `JSON.parse(...) as T` casts, hand-rolled typeguards, or duplicated validators for `gh --json` payloads remain after this work.
2. The validation library is `zod`. `packages/core` takes `zod` as a runtime dependency, matching the existing precedent in `packages/mcp`.
3. When `gh` returns a payload that fails validation, the user sees a single, consistent error that names both the payload shape and the offending field path. Wording is identical regardless of which shipper command triggered the parse (e.g., `gh returned an invalid Issue payload: expected string at labels[0].name`).
4. Validation failures fail hard. Call sites that today swallow `JSON.parse` errors and degrade gracefully (including but not limited to `tryResolvePrForIssue` and `fetchIssueTimelines`) must surface the scoped validation error rather than returning `undefined`/empty. This is a deliberate behavior change.
5. Behavior on well-formed payloads is unchanged. No shipper command changes its user-visible output, flags, or exit codes when `gh` returns payloads it has always returned.
6. The test suite records representative real `gh --json` payloads from this repo's own issues, PRs, checks, timelines, and merge GraphQL responses, commits them as `.json` fixtures, and asserts the parsers accept them. Each parser additionally has unit tests for malformed-payload rejection covering at least: missing required field, wrong type for a required field, wrong shape for a nested object, and wrong shape for an array element.
7. `CLAUDE.md` is updated so that the "All GitHub interaction goes through the `gh` CLI" section directs contributors to the new validation module as the required entry point for parsing any `gh --json` payload.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A single validation module in `packages/core` exports one canonical parser per `gh --json` payload shape currently used by Shipper.
- [ ] No `gh --json`-derived payload anywhere in `packages/core/src`, `packages/cli/src`, or `packages/mcp/src` is consumed via an unvalidated `JSON.parse(...) as T` cast or a bespoke hand-rolled validator after this change.
- [ ] `packages/core/package.json` declares `zod` in `dependencies`.
- [ ] Every existing shipper command passes its current test suite unchanged — happy-path behavior parity on well-formed payloads.
- [ ] Triggering a validation failure (via a fixture with a missing/wrong field) produces the same error wording regardless of which shipper command invoked the parser, and the wording includes both the payload-shape name and the field path.
- [ ] Call sites that previously swallowed `JSON.parse` errors and degraded gracefully (`tryResolvePrForIssue`, `fetchIssueTimelines`, and any other such sites discovered during implementation) now throw the scoped validation error; tests assert the throw.
- [ ] Real `gh --json` fixtures are committed under the repo's test fixtures directory and used by the happy-path tests. Fixtures cover at least: Issue (with labels, comments, author), PR (with labels, reviews), PR checks, issue timeline, PR merge-state view, merge-queue GraphQL response, and any additional shapes pulled in under the full-sweep scope.
- [ ] Each parser has malformed-payload rejection tests covering: missing required field, wrong type for required field, wrong shape for nested object, wrong shape for array element.
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` is updated to document the new validation module as the required entry point for `gh --json` parsing.

# Related Issues

- #637 — Curate `@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core` public API and guard internals with ESLint — **Scope impact (ordering)** — #638 should land first. #638 adds new public exports (zod schemas and parse helpers) and removes ad-hoc cli/mcp parsers, producing the stable surface that #637's public-API curation and ESLint deep-import guards should operate on. Applying `shipper:blocked` to #637 until #638 merges is recommended but is handled outside this grooming session.
- #632 — Reduce CLI command test reliance on wholesale core mocks — **Scope impact** — the fixture format chosen for #638 should be authored with future reuse by a `fakeCore`/`fakeGh` harness in mind so #632 does not need a second fixture format. No blocking relationship.
- #622 — Support token-based GitHub auth for container/CI usage — **Scope impact** — touches the same `packages/core/src/lib/gh.ts` wrapper but does not itself parse `gh --json` output. Merge-level coordination only.

# Out of Scope

- Non-`gh --json` JSON parsing: `packages/core/src/lib/settings.ts`, session files, `reset.ts` comment-stream parsing that is not structurally a `gh --json` payload, `tsup.config.ts` reading `package.json`, desktop `config.ts`/`background.ts` user-config reads.
- Changing `gh` invocation semantics, adding retries, caching, or offline modes.
- Rewriting the control flow of `resolveRef`, `selectIssuesForStage`, or any other consumer — only their parsing step is consolidated.
- Any desktop-side consolidation beyond what already sits in `packages/core`. (`packages/desktop` calls into core.)
- Rolling `zod` out to non-`gh` boundary validation (settings, output protocol, etc.) — that broader direction has been endorsed by product but is deliberately not bundled into this refactor.

# Open Questions

- **Coordination with #637 on public-API classification.** The new validation module must land on the public side of the curated surface #637 introduces. Recommended ordering is #638 → #637; either order works technically but the second issue will need to reconcile.
- Whether the new parsers are pure validators (caller runs `gh` then passes `stdout` in) or combined fetch-and-parse helpers — engineering/design decision.
- Whether `packages/core` should additionally expose a typed `ghJson<T>(args, parser)` helper that wraps invocation + validation, or keep invocation and parsing decoupled — engineering/design decision.

comments.body:
## Product grooming summary

This issue has been product-groomed.

### Cross-issue findings

Three open issues interact with #638; none are hard blockers.

- **#637 — Curate `@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core` public API** — scope impact / ordering. Both issues change the core public surface. Recommending #638 lands first so #637 curates a stable, consolidated boundary and does not need to redo its ESLint deep-import guards.
- **#632 — Reduce CLI command test reliance on wholesale core mocks** — scope impact. The fixture format chosen here should be reusable by a later `fakeCore`/`fakeGh` harness so #632 does not need a second fixture format.
- **#622 — Support token-based GitHub auth** — scope impact only; touches the same `gh.ts` wrapper but does not parse JSON payloads.

### Key decisions

- **Scope: every `gh --json` consumer across `packages/core`, `packages/cli`, and `packages/mcp`.** Expanded from the six shapes originally named. The real sprawl includes `listIssues`, `tryResolvePrForIssue`, `selectIssuesForStage`, `resolveRef`, `reset.ts` PR entries / matching refs, `branch.ts`, PR-files JSON, and `packages/mcp/src/tools.ts` — doing this once avoids a second migration.
- **Validator library: `zod`.** Product owner confirmed zod is the preferred boundary-validation tool going forward. `packages/core` takes zod as a runtime dependency (matching the existing precedent in `packages/mcp`).
- **Error UX: consistent, scoped, field-level errors.** Same wording regardless of which shipper command triggered the parse; includes the payload shape name and the field path (using zod's issue path).
- **Fixtures: real `gh --json` payloads captured from this repo**, committed as `.json` files. Each parser additionally has hand-authored malformed-payload rejection tests (missing required field, wrong type, wrong nested shape, wrong array-element shape).
- **Failure mode: fail hard everywhere.** Deliberate behavior change — sites that today swallow `JSON.parse` errors and degrade gracefully (`tryResolvePrForIssue` returning `undefined`, `fetchIssueTimelines` returning empty) will now throw the scoped validation error. Accepted tradeoff for a single consistent contract.
- **Ordering with #637: block #637 on #638.** #638 itself is not blocked and can proceed now. Applying `shipper:blocked` to #637 is recommended but handled separately, outside this grooming session.
- **Priority: Normal.** Robustness/maintainability refactor; no deadline signal.

### Notable context

- `packages/core` currently declares zero runtime npm dependencies. Adding `zod` is a deliberate change and should be called out in release notes.
- On well-formed payloads, behavior is unchanged. The only user-visible behavior change is in the failure-mode dimension (hard-fail vs. graceful degradation).
- `CLAUDE.md` needs an update so future `gh --json` additions go through the new module by default.

### Decomposition recommendation

**Single PR.** This is a coordinated refactor: the validator module, the `zod` dependency addition, and the call-site rewrites need to land together for the "one canonical validator per shape" invariant to hold at all times. Splitting risks interim states where some call sites are validated and others are not, which reintroduces the divergence this issue exists to eliminate. Fixtures and malformed-payload tests are authored alongside the module rather than as a follow-up.

If the implementing agent discovers the diff is unreviewably large, a reasonable fallback split is:

1. Add the validation module, take `zod` as a core runtime dep, and route `packages/core`'s own `gh --json` consumers through it (Issue, PR, checks, timeline, merge state, GraphQL). Includes all fixtures and malformed-payload tests.
2. Route `packages/cli` and `packages/mcp` `gh --json` consumers through the core validation module. Pure call-site migration; no new validators.

With (2) blocked on (1). Both must land before the "zero unvalidated `gh --json` casts" acceptance criterion is met. Default expectation remains a single PR.

## Verdict

**ACCEPT.** The problem is real. A scan of `packages/core/src`, `packages/cli/src`, and `packages/mcp/src` turned up ~50 `gh --json` consumer sites using at least three distinct parsing styles (unvalidated `JSON.parse(...) as T` casts, hand-rolled `isPlainObject` guards, and newline-delimited-JSON split-and-parse loops). There are ≥15 distinct payload shapes, each parsed in its own way — exactly the divergence the issue wants to eliminate. The grooming's scope, failure-mode choice, fixture format, and single-PR recommendation all stand. Below is the technical design plus my answers to the two open engineering questions.

## Evidence

Representative hand-rolled parsers that this refactor consolidates:

- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:158–181` — `parseIssueData()` (22 lines of field-by-field guards)
- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:183–211` — `parsePRData()` (28 lines, duplicates comment/label/author guards)
- `packages/cli/src/commands/merge.ts:58–127` — `parseGraphQLResponse()` (70 lines, hand-validates nested GraphQL search/pageInfo/nodes/timelineItems)
- `packages/cli/src/commands/merge.ts:129–136` — `parsePRViewData()`
- `packages/core/src/lib/branch.ts:168` — `parseHeadRefName()`

Representative unvalidated casts:

- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:344` — `JSON.parse(json) as RawListIssueData[]`
- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:409` — `JSON.parse(output) as { number; headRefName }[]` (in `tryResolvePrForIssue`, error-swallowing)
- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:504` — `output.split('\n').map((line) => JSON.parse(line) as TimelineLabelEvent)` (in `fetchIssueTimelines`, error-swallowing)
- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:549,576` — `JSON.parse(output) as StageIssueListItem[]` (in `selectIssuesForStage`, error-swallowing — x2)
- `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts:676` — `JSON.parse(output) as { number; body }` (in `resolveRef`, error-swallowing)
- `packages/core/src/lib/merge-execution.ts` — 3 casts to `PRMergeStateViewData`, `{ body }`, `PRStateViewData`
- `packages/core/src/lib/checks.ts:48,73` — `PRChecksLine[]` and `{ jobs: RunViewJob[] }` casts
- `packages/cli/src/commands/merge.ts:162` — `JSON.parse(json) as PRViewResult` (in `fetchPRView`)
- `packages/cli/src/commands/unblock.ts:46,58` — `DependencyIssueData` / `DependencyPrData`
- `packages/cli/src/commands/reset.ts:169`, `packages/cli/src/commands/priority.ts:36`, `packages/cli/src/commands/ship-candidates.ts:125`, `packages/cli/src/commands/ship-execute.ts:181`
- `packages/cli/src/commands/pr-review.ts:62,73`, `packages/cli/src/commands/pr-remediate.ts:214,254,269,360,385,343`
- `packages/mcp/src/tools.ts:70,176`

Platform state verified:

- `packages/core/package.json` declares zero runtime dependencies today. Adding `zod ^3.23.0` (matching the version pinned in `packages/mcp/package.json:33`) is a real change and must be called out in the release notes, as grooming noted.
- `packages/mcp/src/tools.ts:2` already imports `z` from `zod` but only for MCP tool input schemas — there is no existing precedent for validating `gh --json` output with zod.
- The `gh()` wrapper in `packages/core/src/lib/gh.ts` does not touch JSON — it's a pure process spawner with retry. The new validation layer slots in cleanly above it with no changes to invocation semantics.
- No existing test fixture directory. `packages/core/tests/lib/` tests currently inline their JSON as string literals. Introducing a `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/` directory is net-new but consistent with the repo layout.
- CLAUDE.md:48 currently directs contributors to `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts` for GitHub integration. CLAUDE.md:56 states core has zero runtime dependencies. Both lines need to be updated by this PR.

## Design

### Scope boundary

**In scope:** every place that currently calls `JSON.parse()` on `gh` stdout — roughly 30 distinct call sites across the three packages. This includes REST `--json` payloads, GraphQL responses from `gh api graphql`, and newline-delimited-JSON output from `gh api ... --paginate --jq '.[] | {…}'` (the jq filter produces one JSON object per line, each of which is parsed).

**Out of scope (even though the command uses `--json` internally):** jq expressions that reduce to scalars or strings — e.g., `--jq '.defaultBranchRef.name'`, `--jq '.title'`, `--jq '.labels[].name'`, `--jq '.[].id'`. After jq, the output is not JSON; it's plain text. Leave these alone — they're not `gh --json` payload parsing in the spirit of the issue, and forcing zod on them would be ceremony without value. Sites to leave as-is: `resolveBaseBranch`, `branch.ts:generateBranchName`, `lock.ts:isLockStale` and `fetchIssueLabelNames`, `reset.ts:readLabeledEventTime`/`readCommentIds`/`readMatchingRefs`, `repo.ts:getCurrentUser`, `output-protocol/protocol-actions.ts`.

### Module shape

Create **one file**: `packages/core/src/lib/gh-schemas.ts`. Do not split across subdirectories — ~15 shapes and ~100–200 lines of zod don't earn a directory. Organize with section comments grouping Issue, PR, PR-list, PR-view variants, PR-checks, Run-view, Timeline, and GraphQL.

Each shape gets three exports:

```ts
export const IssueSchema = z.object({ /* … */ });
export type Issue = z.infer<typeof IssueSchema>;
export const parseIssue = makeParser(IssueSchema, 'Issue');
```

where `makeParser` is a local helper:

```ts
function makeParser<T>(schema: z.ZodType<T>, shapeName: string): (json: string) => T {
  return (json) => parseGhJson(json, schema, shapeName);
}
```

This gives call sites a named function (`parseIssue(stdout)`) that is self-describing and can't be misused — the shape name is baked in at the module level, so error wording is stable no matter who calls it.

**Answer to open question 1 (pure validator vs combined fetch-and-parse):** expose **both**, but the canonical exports are the pure parsers. The fetch-and-parse helper (`ghJson`) is an optional convenience for the 80% case. This keeps the library composable for multi-step flows (e.g., `resolveRef` tries `pr view`, falls back to `issue view` — that's naturally a two-call sequence, not a single helper) while still giving simple call sites a one-liner.

**Answer to open question 2 (should core expose `ghJson<T>(args, parser)`):** yes. Put it in a new file `packages/core/src/lib/gh-json.ts` (to keep `gh.ts` a pure subprocess wrapper). Re-export both the helper and every schema/parser from `packages/core/src/index.ts`. The CLAUDE.md update then points future contributors at `gh-json.ts` + `gh-schemas.ts` as the required entry points.

### The two helpers

```ts
// packages/core/src/lib/gh-json.ts
import { gh } from './gh.js';

export class GhPayloadError extends Error {
  constructor(
    public readonly shapeName: string,
    public readonly fieldPath: string,
    message: string
  ) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'GhPayloadError';
  }
}

export function parseGhJson<T>(
  json: string,
  schema: z.ZodType<T>,
  shapeName: string
): T {
  let parsed: unknown;
  try {
    parsed = JSON.parse(json);
  } catch (err) {
    throw new GhPayloadError(
      shapeName,
      '',
      `gh returned an invalid ${shapeName} payload: not valid JSON (${toErrorMessage(err)})`
    );
  }
  const result = schema.safeParse(parsed);
  if (!result.success) {
    const issue = result.error.issues[0]!;
    const path = formatZodPath(issue.path); // e.g. "labels[0].name"
    throw new GhPayloadError(
      shapeName,
      path,
      `gh returned an invalid ${shapeName} payload: ${issue.message}${path ? ` at ${path}` : ''}`
    );
  }
  return result.data;
}

export async function ghJson<T>(
  args: string[],
  parse: (json: string) => T,
  options?: { cwd?: string }
): Promise<T> {
  const { stdout } = await gh(args, options);
  return parse(stdout);
}
```

`formatZodPath` converts zod's `(string | number)[]` to `labels[0].name`-style strings. Keep it tiny and local to this file.

### Error wording contract

Exactly: `gh returned an invalid <ShapeName> payload: <zod issue message> at <path>`. Same wording regardless of which command triggered the parse — the shape name is baked into the parser. The PR will include one test (`gh-json.test.ts`) that asserts this format for a representative malformed payload; this is the "consistent wording" acceptance criterion.

### Schemas to define

Fold duplicate variants together where the fields overlap. Recommended surface:

- `IssueSchema` — full `issue view` payload (number, title, state, labels, body, comments, author, createdAt). `labels` and `comments` use shared sub-schemas.
- `PullRequestSchema` — full `pr view` payload (Issue fields + headRefName, baseRefName, reviews).
- `IssueListItemSchema` — list-endpoint item (allows nullable author).
- `IssueStageListItemSchema` — `{ number, title, labels: [{name}] }` for `selectIssuesForStage` and `mcp/tools.ts:listShipperIssuesRaw`.
- `PrSummarySchema` — `{ number, headRefName }` for `tryResolvePrForIssue` and `reset.ts:parsePrEntries`.
- `PrChecksLineSchema` + `RunViewJobsSchema` — for `checks.ts`.
- `PrMergeStateSchema` — `{ mergeStateStatus, mergeable? }`; used by `merge-execution.ts` and `merge.ts:parsePRViewData`. These are the same shape — dedupe them.
- `PrViewBodySchema`, `PrViewStateSchema`, `PrViewCreatedAtSchema`, `PrViewBaseRefSchema`, `PrViewNumberBodySchema` — small single-field views. Consider a single `PrViewPartialSchema` with all fields optional and call-site `.pick()` invocations, but only if the ergonomics are clearly better; individually-named schemas read more cleanly and I'd go that way unless the count balloons.
- `PrViewForMergeSchema` — `{ number, title, headRefName, baseRefName, state, labels: [{name}] }` for `merge.ts:fetchPRView`.
- `IssueStateTitleSchema`, `PrStateMergedTitleSchema` — for `unblock.ts`.
- `IssueLabelsStateSchema` — for `mcp/tools.ts:fetchIssueLabels`.
- `IssueLabelsSchema` — for `ship-execute.ts`.
- `IssueNumberSchema` — `{ number }` used as an existence probe (`resolveRef` issue check, `isPullRequest`). These sites don't use the parsed value — they only care about success/failure — but they should still parse if they read stdout at all. Some don't; leave those alone.
- `IssueWithLabelsBodySchema` — `{ number, title, labels, state, body }` for `reset.ts` and `priority.ts`.
- `TimelineLabelEventSchema` — `{ event, label?: { name }, created_at? }` (validated per-line for the jq-reduced stream).
- `MergeQueueSearchSchema` — the nested GraphQL shape currently built by `parseGraphQLResponse`.
- `PrReviewThreadsSchema` — the GraphQL shape in `pr-remediate.ts`.
- `PrFilesSchema` — `z.array(z.object({ filename: z.string() }))` for `pr-review.ts`.

Where two call sites request strictly different field subsets of the same conceptual shape, prefer two small schemas over one big one with optional fields. Dishonest `.optional()` chains are worse than duplication — a schema should match what `gh` actually returns for that invocation.

### Migration of call sites

Every `JSON.parse(...) as T` at a call site becomes `parseX(stdout)` or `await ghJson(args, parseX)`. Delete every hand-rolled parser function (`parseIssueData`, `parsePRData`, `parseGraphQLResponse`, `parsePRViewData`, `parseHeadRefName`, `parsePrEntries`). Delete the local `interface` declarations that back them — import the `z.infer` type from `gh-schemas.ts` instead.

**Fail-hard surgery** — this is the subtle part. Several call sites today wrap `await gh(...)` AND the subsequent `JSON.parse()` in a single try/catch that degrades on any error. The acceptance criterion only requires validation failures to throw; `gh` invocation failures (network blips, missing repo) can continue to degrade where that's the current behavior. Narrow the try/catch to wrap only `gh()`, and put the `parseX(stdout)` call after the try block so validation errors propagate. Sites to touch:

1. `github.ts:tryResolvePrForIssue` — narrow try/catch to the `gh` call; `parsePrSummaryArray` runs outside.
2. `github.ts:fetchIssueTimelines` — same split; parse each line with `parseTimelineLabelEvent`.
3. `github.ts:selectIssuesForStage` (both gh calls).
4. `github.ts:resolveRef` — the `pr view` branch: narrow to the `gh` call; parse runs outside.
5. `merge-execution.ts:getLinkedIssueNumber`, `isPrMerged`.
6. `unblock.ts:fetchDependencyStates`.
7. `pr-remediate.ts` deadline/base-ref checks.
8. `ship-candidates.ts`, `ship-execute.ts`.
9. Any other site discovered during implementation that matches the pattern — the implementer should grep for `JSON.parse` inside a try/catch that also wraps `gh(`.

### Fixtures

Commit real payloads under `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/`:

- `issue-view.json`, `pr-view.json`, `issue-list.json`, `issue-stage-list.json`, `pr-summary-list.json`, `pr-view-for-merge.json`, `pr-merge-state.json`, `pr-view-body.json`, `pr-view-state.json`, `pr-view-created-at.json`, `pr-view-base-ref.json`, `pr-checks.json`, `run-view-jobs.json`, `issue-timeline-line.json` (single NDJSON line), `issue-with-labels-body.json`, `issue-state-title.json`, `pr-state-merged-title.json`, `issue-labels-state.json`, `pr-files.json`, `merge-queue-search.json`, `pr-review-threads.json`.

Capture these from this repo's own open issues / PRs / actions. The implementer should commit the raw `gh` output verbatim (pretty-printed with `jq` is fine for readability). Fixture files live in source control so they can be regenerated or updated when GitHub changes a schema.

Tests live in `packages/core/tests/lib/gh-schemas.test.ts` and cover:

- One happy-path test per parser, loading the fixture and asserting `parseX(fixture)` returns the expected shape.
- Four malformed tests per parser (derived from the fixture by field surgery): missing required field, wrong type for required field, wrong shape for nested object, wrong shape for array element.
- One cross-cutting test in `packages/core/tests/lib/gh-json.test.ts` asserting error wording format (`gh returned an invalid Issue payload: ... at labels[0].name`).

A separate small test file should assert that the hard-fail-surgery sites (`tryResolvePrForIssue`, `fetchIssueTimelines`, `selectIssuesForStage`, `resolveRef`, `getLinkedIssueNumber`, `isPrMerged`, and their cli/mcp analogues) now throw on malformed payloads where they previously degraded.

### What the implementer must NOT do

- **Do not preserve existing error wording.** `parseIssueData`'s error says `Invalid issue payload.` — that's gone. The new consistent wording is the contract.
- **Do not keep the old hand-rolled parsers as fallbacks.** Delete them. The "one canonical parser per shape" invariant requires their removal.
- **Do not touch non-`gh --json` JSON.** Settings, session files, `tsup.config.ts` package.json reads, desktop config, and jq-reduced scalar outputs are all out of scope. Resist the temptation to migrate settings.ts "while we're here" — grooming explicitly deferred that.
- **Do not add retries, caching, or fallbacks inside `ghJson`.** It's a thin fetch+validate wrapper. `gh()` already handles retries.
- **Do not introduce a `.strict()` or `.passthrough()` policy globally.** Default zod behavior (strip unknown keys) is correct here — GitHub adds fields routinely, and we don't want validation failures on harmless additions. If a specific shape needs `.strict()`, justify it at the schema.
- **Do not split into a two-PR sequence by default.** Grooming already explicitly called single-PR as the target and gave a fallback split only if the diff is unreviewably large. Follow that. Attempt single PR; split only if reviewers ask.
- **Do not re-export the raw zod schemas from cli or mcp.** Consumers outside core should import the parsers and inferred types, not the schema objects — keeps the public API small and stable.

### Test strategy

Every existing test in `packages/core/tests/`, `packages/cli/tests/`, and `packages/mcp/tests/` must continue to pass unmodified on well-formed payloads — this is the behavior-parity guarantee. The only tests that should change are ones that asserted specific legacy error wording from the deleted hand-rolled parsers (e.g., tests asserting `Invalid PR review payload.`) — those update to the new wording. Grep for the old error strings before running the suite to find them.

### CLAUDE.md updates

Two lines change:

- Line 48 (`**GitHub integration:** All GitHub interaction goes through the `gh` CLI. See `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts`.`) — add: "Any new `gh --json` call site must parse its payload through a schema from `packages/core/src/lib/gh-schemas.ts` (or the `ghJson` helper in `gh-json.ts`). Do not use `JSON.parse(...) as T`."
- Line 56 (`the core package uses only Node built-ins at runtime`) — update to: `the core package's only runtime dependency is `zod`; it otherwise uses Node built-ins.`

### Implementation order (for the planning stage)

1. Add `zod` to `packages/core/package.json` dependencies.
2. Create `gh-json.ts` (helper + error class) and `gh-schemas.ts` (schemas + parsers). Add both to the barrel.
3. Commit fixtures under `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/`.
4. Author parser tests (happy-path + 4 malformed per shape) and the error-wording test.
5. Migrate core call sites — `github.ts`, `checks.ts`, `merge-execution.ts`, `branch.ts`. Apply the fail-hard narrowing surgery.
6. Migrate cli call sites — `merge.ts` (including deleting `parseGraphQLResponse` and `parsePRViewData`), `unblock.ts`, `priority.ts`, `reset.ts`, `ship-candidates.ts`, `ship-execute.ts`, `pr-review.ts`, `pr-remediate.ts`.
7. Migrate mcp call sites — `tools.ts`.
8. Update CLAUDE.md.
9. Run `npm run build`, `npm run type-check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test` from the repo root.

Verified complete when `grep -rn "JSON.parse" packages/core/src packages/cli/src packages/mcp/src` returns no hits on `gh` output — only on non-`gh` sources (settings, session files, etc.).

# Implementation Plan

## Overview

This refactor centralizes every structured GitHub CLI JSON parse into `packages/core`, using `zod` as the single runtime validation boundary and exposing canonical parser functions through the core public API. The work should migrate core, CLI, and MCP consumers in dependency order, preserve all happy-path behavior, and deliberately change malformed-payload handling so validation failures surface as the same scoped `GhPayloadError` everywhere.

## Steps

### Step 1: Add the core validation entry points and public exports

- **File**: `packages/core/package.json`, `packages/core/src/lib/gh-json.ts`, `packages/core/src/lib/gh-schemas.ts`, `packages/core/src/index.ts`
- **Current state**: `packages/core/package.json` has no runtime dependencies; `packages/core/src/lib/gh.ts` only wraps subprocess execution and retry; there is no shared gh-payload parser module; `packages/core/src/index.ts` does not export any JSON validation helper or schema surface.
- **Changes**:
  - Add `"zod": "^3.23.0"` to `packages/core/package.json` `dependencies`, matching the existing version in `packages/mcp/package.json`.
  - Create `packages/core/src/lib/gh-json.ts` with the shared parsing primitives: a `GhPayloadError` class, `formatZodPath()`, and a `parseGhJson()` helper that performs `JSON.parse`, runs `schema.safeParse`, and throws `gh returned an invalid <Shape> payload: ...` messages with the shape name and field path.
  - Create `packages/core/src/lib/gh-schemas.ts` with shared sub-schemas (`author`, `label`, `comment`, `review`, GraphQL `pageInfo`, timeline event, matching ref, comment-id line) plus one canonical schema/parser pair per currently used gh payload shape. Keep export naming consistent, e.g. `IssueSchema`/`parseIssue`, `PrMergeStateViewSchema`/`parsePrMergeStateView`.
  - Re-export `gh-json.ts` and `gh-schemas.ts` from `packages/core/src/index.ts` so `packages/cli` and `packages/mcp` can delete their local GH payload interfaces and parsers instead of deep-importing internals.
- **Why**: This establishes the single validation layer and error contract before any call-site rewrites start.

### Step 2: Replace the core GitHub helper parsers and hard-fail the existing graceful parse fallbacks

- **File**: `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts`
- **Current state**: This file owns `parseIssueData()`, `parsePRData()`, `parseAuthor()`, `parseLabels()`, `parseComments()`, and `parseReviews()`, and also does unvalidated parsing in `listIssues()`, `tryResolvePrForIssue()`, `fetchIssueTimelines()`, `selectIssuesForStage()`, and `resolveRef()`. Several of those functions currently swallow parse errors and return `undefined`, `[]`, or generic command-specific errors.
- **Changes**:
  - Delete the local GH payload interfaces and parser helpers that duplicate the new core schemas, and import the canonical parser/type exports from `gh-schemas.ts`.
  - Route `fetchIssue()` and `fetchPR()` through the new issue/PR parsers, leaving `formatIssue()` and `formatPR()` unchanged.
  - Replace the raw `JSON.parse(...) as T` calls in `listIssues()`, `tryResolvePrForIssue()`, `fetchIssueTimelines()`, `selectIssuesForStage()`, and `resolveRef()` with canonical parsers for the list-item array, PR-summary array, timeline-event line, stage-issue array, and `{ number, body }` PR view shapes.
  - Narrow every `try/catch` that currently wraps both `gh()` and parsing so it catches only the `gh()` invocation. The parser call must happen after the `try` block so `GhPayloadError` propagates unchanged; only transport/auth/not-found failures should keep the existing warn/fallback behavior.
  - Keep all existing happy-path logic intact: branch-name matching in `tryResolvePrForIssue()`, FIFO ordering by timeline label time, stale-lock inclusion and cleanup, and linked-issue extraction from PR bodies.
- **Why**: `github.ts` is the main concentration of duplicated parsers and the exact place where the issue requires parse failures to stop degrading into silent fallbacks.

### Step 3: Sweep the remaining core GH consumers

- **File**: `packages/core/src/lib/checks.ts`, `packages/core/src/lib/merge-execution.ts`, `packages/core/src/lib/branch.ts`, `packages/core/src/lib/reset.ts`
- **Current state**: `checks.ts` casts the `gh pr checks` array and `gh run view --json jobs` object; `merge-execution.ts` parses merge-state, PR body, and PR state inside broad catches; `branch.ts` has a bespoke `parseHeadRefName()` helper; `reset.ts` owns `parsePrEntries()`, `parseMatchingRefs()`, and inline NDJSON parsing for `{ id, created_at }` comment records.
- **Changes**:
  - In `checks.ts`, replace both casts with canonical parsers for the checks array and jobs object, but leave enrichment, failed-step extraction, and log dump naming untouched.
  - In `merge-execution.ts`, replace the local payload interfaces with the canonical merge-state, PR-body, and PR-state parsers. Split the existing `try/catch` blocks so parse failures from `getMergeStateStatus()`, `getLinkedIssueNumber()`, and `isPrMerged()` throw instead of being rewritten as generic fetch warnings.
  - In `branch.ts`, delete `parseHeadRefName()` and parse the `headRefName` view payload through the shared parser; preserve the existing branch-discovery behavior and warning messages for fetch failures.
  - In `reset.ts`, replace `parsePrEntries()`, `parseMatchingRefs()`, and the `{ id, created_at }` per-line parse with canonical parsers. Do not touch jq-scalar outputs such as raw comment IDs or stage timestamps, because they are intentionally out of scope.
- **Why**: This finishes the `packages/core` migration and gives CLI/MCP a stable, parser-backed public API to consume.

### Step 4: Migrate the CLI’s workflow-specific GH parsers

- **File**: `packages/cli/src/commands/merge.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/pr-remediate.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/pr-review.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/reset.ts`
- **Current state**: `merge.ts` defines `parseGraphQLResponse()`, `parsePRViewData()`, and an unvalidated `fetchPRView()` cast; `pr-remediate.ts` hand-validates `{ createdAt }` and casts `{ baseRefName }`; `pr-review.ts` locally parses the paginated PR-files response from `gh api ... --paginate --slurp`; CLI `reset.ts` has its own `parseIssueViewData()` implementation.
- **Changes**:
  - In `merge.ts`, delete `SearchNode`, `GraphQLResponse`, `PRViewData`, `parseGraphQLResponse()`, and `parsePRViewData()`, and replace them with imports of the new core merge-queue GraphQL parser, ready-PR view parser, and merge-state view parser. Keep the queue traversal, FIFO sorting, dry-run flow, and shared merge-helper calls unchanged.
  - In `pr-remediate.ts`, replace `parseCreatedAt()` and the `JSON.parse(baseBranchStdout)` cast with the canonical `createdAt` and `baseRefName` parsers so timing/base-branch validation failures bubble with the shared wording.
  - In `pr-review.ts`, replace the `Array<Array<{ filename: string }>>` cast with a canonical parser for the slurped PR-files payload before flattening and writing `pr-files.json`.
  - In CLI `reset.ts`, delete `parseIssueViewData()` and use the same issue-state/labels parser that `next.ts`, `priority.ts`, and MCP will consume.
- **Why**: These commands currently own the last large CLI-specific parser implementations, including the GraphQL merge queue payload called out in the issue requirements.

### Step 5: Migrate the remaining CLI and MCP consumers that still cast gh output locally

- **File**: `packages/cli/src/commands/adopt.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/issue-list.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/next.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/priority.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/ship-candidates.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/ship-merge.ts`, `packages/cli/src/commands/unblock.ts`, `packages/mcp/src/tools.ts`
- **Current state**: Each of these files still has local GH payload interfaces plus `JSON.parse(...) as T`, and several catches currently turn malformed successful stdout into “not found”, empty arrays, or warning-only behavior.
- **Changes**:
  - Replace each local GH payload interface with imports from `@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core`, and remove any now-unused `isPlainObject` imports or parser helpers.
  - In `adopt.ts`, `issue-list.ts`, `next.ts`, `priority.ts`, `ship-candidates.ts`, and `ship-merge.ts`, narrow `try/catch` blocks so command-specific fallback behavior stays attached to `gh()` execution failures only; successful-but-invalid JSON must now throw `GhPayloadError`.
  - In `unblock.ts`, keep the intended control flow where issue lookup failures render an “Unknown” dependency and PR lookup failures mean “treat this ref as an issue”, but only for failed `gh()` calls. Parse successful issue/PR payloads through canonical dependency parsers before rendering the markdown context file.
  - In `packages/mcp/src/tools.ts`, replace `listShipperIssuesRaw()` and `fetchIssueLabels()` casts with the same core parsers used by CLI `issue-list`/`next` so MCP tool failures match the CLI contract.
- **Why**: This closes the cross-package migration and satisfies the “no bespoke gh JSON parser remains in core/cli/mcp” acceptance criterion.

### Step 6: Commit the real GH fixtures that back the parser suite

- **File**: `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-view.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-view.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-list.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-stage-list.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-summary-list.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-view-for-merge.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-merge-state.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-view-body.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-view-state.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-view-created-at.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-view-base-ref.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-checks.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/run-view-jobs.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-timeline-line.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-with-labels-body.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-state-title.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/issue-labels-state.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-state-merged-title.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-files.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/merge-queue-search.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/pr-review-threads.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/matching-refs.json`, `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/comment-id-created-at-line.json`
- **Current state**: `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/` does not exist today, and the current tests inline representative GH payloads directly in the test files.
- **Changes**:
  - Create the new tracked fixture directory and add one real payload per canonical parser shape, using raw output captured from this repo’s own `gh issue view`, `gh pr view`, `gh pr checks`, `gh run view`, `gh api graphql`, `gh api ... --paginate --slurp`, and jq-reduced NDJSON calls already present in the codebase.
  - Keep fixture filenames aligned with parser names so the parser test tables can map `parseX` to `x.json` mechanically.
  - Store jq-reduced NDJSON examples as single-line `.json` files for the per-line parsers instead of inventing synthetic wrapper arrays.
- **Why**: The acceptance criteria require committed real fixtures, and the parser suite needs stable inputs that future issues can also reuse.

### Step 7: Add dedicated parser-contract tests in core

- **File**: `packages/core/tests/lib/gh-schemas.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/lib/gh-json.test.ts`
- **Current state**: There is no dedicated schema test module or shared error-contract test; parser assertions are spread across feature tests like `github.test.ts`, `checks.test.ts`, `branch.test.ts`, and `merge-execution.test.ts`.
- **Changes**:
  - Create `gh-schemas.test.ts` as a table-driven suite that loads each fixture with `readFileSync(new URL('../fixtures/gh/<name>.json', import.meta.url), 'utf-8')`, runs the happy-path parser, and then mutates the parsed object into four invalid variants per parser: missing required field, wrong required type, wrong nested object shape, and wrong array element shape.
  - Create `gh-json.test.ts` to lock the shared `GhPayloadError` message contract for both invalid JSON and schema-validation failures, including shape name and dotted/bracketed field paths.
  - Keep these tests focused on the validation layer only; use the existing feature tests for command behavior and control-flow assertions.
- **Why**: This gives the new validation layer its own acceptance suite and prevents the error wording from drifting across callers.

### Step 8: Update the affected feature tests and contributor documentation

- **File**: `packages/core/tests/lib/github.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/lib/checks.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/lib/merge-execution.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/lib/branch.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/merge.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/pr-remediate.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/pr-review.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/reset.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/adopt.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/issue-list.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/next.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/priority.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/ship-candidates.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/commands/unblock.test.ts`, `packages/mcp/tests/tools.test.ts`, `CLAUDE.md`
- **Current state**: The existing tests assert legacy parser helpers, raw `SyntaxError` behavior, or graceful parse fallbacks; `CLAUDE.md` still points contributors at `packages/core/src/lib/github.ts` for GitHub integration and says core has only Node built-ins at runtime.
- **Changes**:
  - Update the core feature tests so malformed successful gh responses now throw the shared validation error in the sites that used to swallow parse failures: `tryResolvePrForIssue()`, `fetchIssueTimelines()`, `selectIssuesForStage()`, `getLinkedIssueNumber()`, `isPrMerged()`, and `getBranchForPR()`.
  - Update the CLI and MCP tests so malformed payloads from successful gh commands reject with the shared `GhPayloadError` wording instead of warning and returning fallback values. In particular, replace the current `ship-candidates.test.ts` expectation that invalid blocked-issue JSON returns `[]`, and stop asserting raw `SyntaxError` in `branch.test.ts`.
  - Leave happy-path command expectations intact; the goal is behavior parity on valid payloads and hard-fail behavior only on malformed ones.
  - Update `CLAUDE.md` so the GitHub integration section points contributors to `packages/core/src/lib/gh-json.ts` and `packages/core/src/lib/gh-schemas.ts` as the required entry points for any future structured gh payload parsing, and update the runtime-dependency note to say core’s only runtime dependency is now `zod`.
- **Why**: This makes the new contract executable across the repo and documents the invariant so future `gh` integrations do not reintroduce ad-hoc parsing.

## Verification

1. Run `npm run lint` and confirm it passes.
2. Run `npm run format:check` and confirm it passes.
3. Run `npm run type-check` and confirm it passes.
4. Run `npm run build` and confirm it passes.
5. Run `npm run test` and confirm it passes.
6. Confirm `packages/core/package.json` now declares `zod` in `dependencies`, with no new GH-payload validation dependency added to `packages/cli` or `packages/mcp`.
7. Confirm `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/` contains the committed real fixtures for issue view, PR view, PR checks, issue timeline, PR merge-state view, merge-queue GraphQL response, PR review threads, PR files, and the other shapes migrated in this sweep.
8. Run `rg -n "JSON.parse\\(|parseIssueData|parsePRData|parseGraphQLResponse|parsePRViewData|parseHeadRefName|parsePrEntries" packages/core/src packages/cli/src packages/mcp/src` and confirm any remaining hits are only out-of-scope non-gh parsing sites or the centralized validation layer, not a gh payload consumer.
9. Run the new parser-focused tests and confirm every parser accepts its real fixture and rejects the four malformed variants with the same `gh returned an invalid <Shape> payload: ... at <path>` format regardless of caller.
10. Confirm the regression tests for `tryResolvePrForIssue`, `fetchIssueTimelines`, `selectIssuesForStage`, `getLinkedIssueNumber`, `isPrMerged`, `selectBlockedIssues`, the dependency lookups in `unblock.ts`, and the MCP issue-list / issue-label lookups now throw the shared validation error on malformed successful gh output instead of returning `undefined`, `[]`, or “not found”.
11. Confirm existing happy-path command tests across `packages/core/tests`, `packages/cli/tests`, and `packages/mcp/tests` still pass without user-visible output, flag, or exit-code changes when fixtures contain valid payloads.

## Notes for implementer

- Keep `gh.ts` as a pure subprocess/retry wrapper. The new validation logic belongs in `gh-json.ts` and the schema module, not inside the command runner.
- Do not validate jq-scalar outputs such as `.defaultBranchRef.name`, `.title`, `.labels[].name`, or raw ID streams; this issue is only about structured JSON and per-line JSON objects that the code currently parses.
- Use zod’s default object behavior unless a specific shape truly requires stricter handling. GitHub adds fields frequently, and harmless extra keys should not break the parser contract.
- When preserving existing fallback behavior for transport failures, split the `try/catch` around `gh()` only. Do not catch and rewrap `GhPayloadError`, or the cross-command wording requirement will fail.
- `packages/core/src/lib/gh-json.ts`, `packages/core/src/lib/gh-schemas.ts`, `packages/core/tests/lib/gh-json.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/lib/gh-schemas.test.ts`, and `packages/core/tests/fixtures/gh/` are all new tracked paths today and are not gitignored.

## Agent Feedback

- The repository guidance in `AGENTS.md` describes a three-package monorepo but omits `packages/mcp`, even though this issue’s scope explicitly includes MCP. Keeping that overview in sync would make planning sweeps like this less error-prone.

title: Consolidate gh CLI JSON parsing into a single validation layer
